name: requirements-elicitation
description: Requirements gathering and elicitation techniques for business analysis. Use when conducting stakeholder interviews, gathering functional/non-functional requirements, or identifying gaps in requirements.
capabilities: Guide stakeholder interviews using structured question templates organized by category
Apply FURPS+ model to gather non-functional requirements (performance, security, usability)
Use MoSCoW method and value-effort matrix for requirement prioritization
Identify functional and non-functional requirements with SMART criteria validation
Conduct gap analysis between current and desired system states
Generate stakeholder maps with influence and interest level analysis
author: DoubleslashSE
securitySummary: All 25 static findings are false positives. This skill is pure documentation containing only markdown templates for requirements gathering. No executable code, no network calls, no file system access, and no command execution capabilities exist. The scanner incorrectly flagged compliance terminology, markdown formatting syntax, and the FURPS+ model acronym as security patterns.
valueStatement: Poor requirements lead to project failures and wasted development effort. This skill provides structured question templates and techniques to systematically gather complete, unambiguous requirements from stakeholders through proven elicitation methods.
limitations: Does not execute code or access external systems to automatically extract requirements
Does not generate formal requirement documents in specific organizational formats
Does not integrate with project management tools to create tickets automatically

useCases.title: Conduct stakeholder interviews
useCases.description: Use structured question templates to gather complete requirements from stakeholders during discovery phase.

useCases.title: Define MVP scope
useCases.description: Apply prioritization techniques to identify must-have features for minimum viable product releases.

useCases.title: Validate requirements quality
useCases.description: Check gathered requirements against SMART and INVEST criteria before development begins.

promptTemplates.title: Basic interview start
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me conduct a requirements gathering interview. The stakeholder is [role] and we are discussing [project]. Start with open-ended questions to understand their needs.

promptTemplates.title: Functional requirements
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the main functional requirements for [system]? Ask me about user actions, data processing, business rules, and expected outputs.

promptTemplates.title: Non-functional requirements
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me gather non-functional requirements using the FURPS+ model. Ask me about performance, security, usability, reliability, and supportability needs.

promptTemplates.title: Prioritization workshop
promptTemplates.prompt: We have gathered [list requirements]. Help me apply the MoSCoW method to prioritize each one as Must Have, Should Have, Could Have, or Won't Have.

bestPractices: Start interviews with open-ended questions before moving to specific details
Validate understanding by summarizing requirements back to stakeholders
Use follow-up patterns (specific examples, break down complexity) when answers are vague or incomplete
antiPatterns: Asking leading questions that suggest expected answers
Skipping validation questions to confirm understanding
Accepting requirements without checking for ambiguity or conflicts

faq.question: What tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides guidance and templates rather than direct tool integration.

faq.question: How many questions should I ask in one session?
faq.answer: Plan for 60-90 minutes per stakeholder category. Break longer interviews into multiple sessions to avoid stakeholder fatigue.

faq.question: Can I customize the question templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates are starting points. Adjust questions based on stakeholder domain knowledge and project context.

faq.question: Is my interview data stored anywhere?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides guidance. All interview data remains in your conversation with the AI assistant.

faq.question: Why use FURPS+ instead of other NFR frameworks?
faq.answer: FURPS+ provides a comprehensive checklist covering Functionality, Usability, Reliability, Performance, Supportability, and constraints.

faq.question: How does this differ from requirements management tools?
faq.answer: This skill guides the elicitation process. Requirements management tools organize and track requirements after they are gathered.
